stories.title: Neville: Arsenal will need wheelbarrow to get over line in PL title race
stories.description: Sky Sports' Gary Neville says Arsenal "will need a wheelbarrow" to get over the line in the Premier League title race after an edgy 1-0 win against Newcastle on Saturday Night Football.

stories.title: Starmer stands by sacking of senior civil servant - and signals intention to fight on
stories.description: Sir Keir Starmer has defended his decision to sack Olly Robbins over the vetting process for Peter Mandelson - saying the senior civil servant only faced the "everyday pressure of government".

stories.title: Strait talk: What's at stake in Hormuz
stories.description: The Strait of Hormuz, which is a key passage for oil produced in the Middle East, has come to the forefront of the Iran-US conflict. We get analysis from Duncan L Potts, a former senior Royal Navy officer.

stories.title: A difficult balance to find: Pakistan wants to be key actor in shaping US-Iran peace
stories.description: Since the beginning of the Iran-US war, Pakistan has tried to host talks between the two countries, aiming at finding peace. We hear from Pakistani policy analyst and journalist Raza Rumi.

stories.title: Race for Europe: Advantage Brighton after Brentford lose at Man Utd
stories.description: The Premier League could have six teams in the Champions League next season and 10 in Europe after a potentially pivotal weekend.

stories.title: Festive mood in West Bank as local elections underway
stories.description: A festive and cheerful mood has taken hold of the West Bank as the Palestinian Authority holds local elections, FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky said, reporting from Bethlehem. Most electoral lists are aligned with president Mahmud Abbas's secular-nationalist Fatah party or running as independents. There are no lists affiliated with Fatah's archrival Hamas, which controls nearly half of the Gaza Strip.

stories.title: Low voter turnout in Gaza as West Bank holds local elections
stories.description: Palestinians in the West Bank and central Gaza voted Saturday in municipal elections, the first since the war in Gaza erupted, with low turnout and a limited political field. Nearly 1.5 million people are registered to vote in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, as well as 70,000 people in Gaza's Deir el-Balah area, according to the Ramallah-based Central Elections Commission.

stories.title: Iran rules out talks with US unless blockade of ports lifted
stories.description: US envoys were expected in the Pakistani capital on Saturday to kickstart a new round of peace negotiations with Iran, although Iranian state media said Tehran's representatives had no immediate plans to hold face-to-face talks. "According to Tehran, until and unless that blockade (of Iranian ports) is lifted, the conditions are not ripe for diplomacy," FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah said, reporting from Tehran.

stories.title: Starmer faces mounting pressure as Labour leadership chatter grows
stories.description: On Friday, Keir Starmer's circle gathered at his country retreat of Chequers to wargame the coming weeks for a prime minister whose future is on the line.

stories.title: Elections in West Bank provide 'credibility to Palestinian sovereignty', expert says
stories.description: Local elections are being held in the West Bank and parts of Gaza on Saturday. The vote will determine the makeup of local councils tasked with overseeing water, roads, and electricity. Bertrand Besancenot, former French ambassador to Qatar and Saudi Arabia, said the vote would provide “credibility to Palestinian sovereignty” if voter turnout is high.

stories.title: Elections in West Bank: ‘Barometer’ on Palestinian public opinion
stories.description: Palestinians on Saturday cast ballots in tents and damaged buildings in Deir al-Balah, marking the first local election in part of Gaza Strip in over 20 years. With Hamas affiliated candidates on the list of candidates, the vote serves as a "barometer" on Palestinian public opinion, said Hussein Ibish, Senior Resident Scholar at the Arab Gulf States Institute.

stories.title: Displaced Lebanese remain skeptical despite extended truce
stories.description: In the coastal city of Sidon, displaced families sheltering from months of conflict express little relief at the extension of a fragile truce between Lebanon and Israel. While Donald Trump voiced optimism about a path toward peace, many civilians – uprooted by fighting involving Hezbollah – say ongoing strikes, uncertainty, and deep mistrust prevent them from returning home or believing in lasting stability.

stories.title: US envoys to travel to Islamabad despite Iran ruling out direct negotiations with US
stories.description: US envoys are expected to travel to Pakistan on Saturday in a new bid to salvage ceasefire talks with Tehran, even as Iran ruled out direct negotiations with US representatives as its top diplomat arrived in Islamabad. The latest effort to broker a deal comes as an indefinite ceasefire has paused most fighting, but the economic fallout is still mounting with global energy shipments disrupted by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Spain PM brushes of reported Pentagon email on punishing NATO members
stories.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Friday refused to be drawn into a dispute with the United States over reports that the Pentagon is weighing whether to punish members of NATO that fail to support American operations in the Iran war. Among those in the firing line is Spain, which has refused to allow US forces involved in the war to use bases on its territory or airspace. Spain says that US-Israeli actions in the Iran war contravenes international law.

stories.title: Mojtaba Khamenei remains out of the spotlight in Iran
stories.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has remained out of the spotlight since being named supreme leader of Iran, fuelling speculation that he was seriously injured in the strike that killed his father almost two months ago. According to a New York Times report, Khamenei lives in hiding, fearing for his life and participates in decision making by hand-written notes.

stories.title: В Вашингтоне перед визитом Карла III по ошибке вывесили австралийские флаги
stories.description: В Вашингтоне перед визитом Карла III по ошибке вывесили австралийские флаги

stories.title: چاپ کتابی از روایت های زندگی رهبر شهید انقلاب به زبان روسی
stories.description: با همکاری رایزنی فرهنگی جمهوری اسلامی ایران در فدراسیون روسیه، کتاب «پدر دلسوز» حاوی روایت‌هایی از زندگی رهبر شهیدمان به زبان روسی منتشر شد.

stories.title: Vučić rekao da će SNS imati skup u Beogradu na Vidovdan, ili...
stories.description: "Biće to vrijeme važnih i velikih odluka za građane Srbije, ali da li su to izbori ili nešto drugo, otom potom", izjavio je Vučić u Parizu, gdje učestvuje na Međunarodnoj konferenciji o globalnoj politici.

stories.title: پیام‌رسان ایکس از راه رسید
stories.description: پیام‌رسان ایکس از راه رسید

stories.title: Live η επίσκεψη Μητσοτάκη - Μακρόν στη φρεγάτα «Κίμων» στον Πειραιά
stories.description: Στην επίσκεψη συμμετέχουν επίσης οι υπουργοί Άμυνας Ελλάδας και Γαλλίας, οι υφυπουργοί Εξωτερικών των δύο χωρών, καθώς και η στρατιωτική ηγεσία της χώρας Αμέσως μετά οι υπογραφές για τη νέα αμυντική συνεργασία Ελλάδας Γαλλίας.
